default: {@i (a)} two {@item club|PHB|clubs}, {@i (b)} a {@item greatclub|PHB}, or {@i (c)} a {@item quarterstaff|PHB}
{@i (a)} a {@item shortbow|PHB} and 20 {@item arrow|PHB|arrows}, or {@i (b)} four {@item javelin|PHB|javelins}
{@item leather armor|PHB}, a {@item dagger|PHB}, and an {@item explorer's pack|PHB}

entries.name: Shifter
entries: The especially stout and hairy dwarf disappeared in a flash of emerald light and dust, and where he had stood was a massive ram, twice as tall as the dwarf. Charging forward, the brutish beast ran roughshod over the goblin bandits that were attempting to pillage this remote farm. As the would-be thieves fled into the forest, a similar flash of verdant light shone between the trees, and the wild dwarf walked out and returned to his dwelling.

In the chaos of battle, the primal call of the hunt was almost irresistible. In one moment, she was the fierce lioness, tearing into the troll's vile flesh. In the next, she was a humble rodent darting unnoticed in the confusion of combat. As the dust settled, she returned to the shape of a wild female elf, what she knew to be her true shape. But even as she regained her composure, part of her deep within longed to eat of the carnage surrounding her.

The bandit guard couldn't help but think that there was something different about the bird that stared at him from the window. It was as if it was watching where he had left the key to the cells below. When the guard left, the same bird transformed into a primitive young boy, picked up the key, and lept out of that same window. That night, there would be a jailbreak from the baron's most secure dungeon.
All three of the warriors described here are considered Shifters, warriors imbued with primal bestial powers that let them change their forms, taking on the shapes of wild Beasts.

entries.name: Primal Companion
entries: While many claim a deep understanding of the natural world, none hold a deeper connection than those known as Shifters. These rare warrior sages live among beasts in the wilderness and develop primal powers of their own. For many, the mystic power of this bond infuses their blood with incredible bestial power, enhancing their senses and changing their form.

Drawing upon this primal connection to the wild, Shifters can communicate with beasts as if they were one of the pack. Their own physical forms are changed, and they gain savage claws or fangs and thickened skin. This primeval bond is also the source of their signature ability, the power to transform themselves, taking on the physical form of wild beasts.

entries.name: Many Forms
entries: The mystical ability that flows in their blood allows Shifters to take on the form of any animal that they have bonded with. Though each Shifter has a certain family of beasts they favor, and instinctively learn to transform into beasts in that family, all Shifters can learn the shape of any animal they encounter.

For many Shifters, the struggle between their own rational intellect and the savage instincts of the beasts they transform into is core to their being. Ignore their instincts and dull their mystical power, but give too much and risk losing their self.

entries.name: Creating Your Shifter
entries: When creating your Shifter, the most important thing for you to decide is how they gained their mystical abilities. Are you the offspring of an elder Archdruid or lycanthrope, born with an instinctive ability to change your form? Were you blessed by an Archfey or powerful Celestial? Or, were you born in the wild and developed your mystical gift independently?

Also, though a Shifter can transform into any Beast they have seen before, each Shifter has a favored beast shape that matches their personality. Do you favor a powerful predatory beast, matching your willingness to fight? A small rodent that can remain hidden and unnoticed? Or, a cold-blooded reptile that is willing to make the utilitarian decisions to survive?

entries.name: Quick Build
entries: You can make a Shifter quickly by using these suggestions: Make Strength or Dexterity your highest ability score, then your Constitution. Second, choose the {@background Hermit} background.

classFeature.name: Bloodline
entries: At 1st level, you choose the Bloodline from the list below that best represents your primal abilities and favored wild Beasts.
Your Bloodline grants you features at 1st level, and again when you reach 7th, 13th, and 18th level in this class. Your Bloodline features all use your Shifter save DC (see below).

entries.name: Bloodline Shapes
entries: Each Bloodline has an instinctive connection to certain types of Beasts; these are known as their Bloodline Shapes. Beast stat blocks in the {@loader Compendium of Beasts|creature/LaserLlama; Compendium of Beasts.json} will include which Bloodline (or Bloodlines) they belong to.
For other Beasts, work with your DM to determine which of the seven Bloodlines, if any, other Beasts may belong to.

entries.name: Magical Taxonomy
entries: While animals in the real world are organized into a complex taxonomy, there are only six Bloodlines that the mystical powers of a Shifter use: {@i Aquatic}, {@i Avian}, {@i Brute}, {@i Carnivore}, {@i Insect}, {@i Reptilian}, and {@i Vermin}.
Though, legends speak of Shifters who call on the powers of an {@i Ancient Bloodline} of Beasts...

classFeature.name: Wild Shape
entries: Also at 1st level, you gain the mystical ability to change your form, Shifting into Beast Shapes, following the rules below:

entries.name: Beast Shapes
entries: At 1st level, you know one {@filter Beast Shape of your choice of CR 0 or lower|bestiary|type=Beast|challenge rating=[&0;&0]|source=LLCompendiumOfBeasts} from your Bloodline Shapes. As a bonus action, you can Shift to take the form of a Beast Shape you know.

When you Shift, your statistics are replaced by those of the Beast Shape, but you retain your alignment, personality, {@u hit points}, and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. You also retain all of your skill and saving throw proficiencies, in addition to gaining those of the Beast. If you and your Beast Shape both share a proficiency, use the higher bonus.

You revert to your normal form if you are Incapacitated or if you use your bonus action to do so. You can Shift from one Beast Shape directly into another. However, after you spend 1 hour in Beast Shapes without reverting to your normal form, you will instantly revert to your normal form.

entries.name: Diminutive Beasts
entries: Some of the Beast Shapes referenced by this class are a new creature size: {@u Diminutive}. If you take {@u any} damage or you fail a saving throw while Shifted into a Diminutive Beast Shape, you instantly revert back to your normal form.

entries.name: Equipment
entries: When you Shift, you can choose for any natural or unrefined equipment, such as objects made from wood, animal parts, or other non-refined material, to merge with your Beast Shape.
However, any equipment made from refined metal or other highly processed materials, such as metal weapons or armor, falls to the ground in your space when you Shift.

entries.name: Features & Spellcasting
entries: When you Shift, you retain the benefits of features from your class, race, and other sources, and can still use them so long as that Beast is physically capable of doing so. However, you lose all the benefits from special senses, such as Darkvision, unless your Beast Shape also has that sense.
You can't cast spells in Beast Shape, but you can maintain your concentration on spells that you cast before you Shifted.

entries.name: Mystic Empowerment
entries: The primal power in your blood draws out the full potential of your Beasts. When you Shift, you can use the Shifter save DC and Shifter attack modifier in place of the Beast's innate save DC and its attack modifier, unless the Beast's bonus is higher. They use the Constitution modifeir of your normal form:

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: Unless your Beast Shape's AC was already higher, you can use the Natural Armor AC from your Bloodline while in Beast Shape. If you do so, use your normal form's ability scores.

entries.name: Learning New Beast Shapes
entries: At certain Shifter levels, you instinctively learn to Shift into more powerful Beasts, as shown in the Max. CR column of the Shifter table. Every time your Max. CR increases, you instinctively learn one Bloodline Shape of your choice equal to that new CR, or lower.

classFeature.name: Primal Bond
entries: At 2nd level, your mystical bond with the wild grows. You can verbally communicate with and understand Beasts. This does not mean Beasts are automatically Friendly to you. When you communicate with a Beast, its awareness and ability to recall knowledge is limited by its Intelligence score.

You can also draw upon this bond to learn additional Beast Shapes. As an action, you can touch a live, non-Hostile Beast within your Max. CR limit, and learn its Beast Shape. There is no limit to the number of Beast Shapes you can learn.

classFeature.name: Bestial Instincts
entries: Your instincts allow you to quickly react to danger. Beginning at 3rd level, so long as you are not Incapacitated or Surprised, you have advantage on your initiative rolls, and when you roll initiative, you can Shift into a Beast Shape.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 4th level, you can increase one of your ability scores by 2, or two ability scores by 1. You cannot use this feature to increase any ability score above 20. You gain this feature again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level.

classFeature.name: Feral Warrior
entries: At 5th level, the power in your blood awakens the ferocity that lay dormant within you, granting you the following benefits:

items: Whenever you use your action to Dash or Disengage, you can make one Natural Weapon attack as a bonus action.
When you take the Attack action in your normal form, you can make two Natural Weapon attacks instead of one.
When you are in Beast Shape, you can use two Actions in the Beast's statblock instead of one. If the Beast has the Multiattack action, you can only use it once per turn.

classFeature.name: Mystical Strikes
entries: Also at 5th level, your Natural Weapon attacks count as magical for overcoming Resistances and Immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

classFeature.name: Adrenaline Surge
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can draw on your primal drive to survive in the midst of battle. Whenever you take damage, you can use a reaction to gain temporary hit points equal to the damage taken. However, these temporary hit points only last for 1 minute.
You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your normal form's Constitution modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all uses at the end of each short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Bloodline Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Bloodline.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 8th level, you can increase one of your ability scores by 2, or two ability scores by 1. You cannot use this feature to increase any ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Wild Awareness
entries: At 9th level, your mystical abilities grant you an instinctive knowledge of your surroundings. When you finish a short or long rest, you gain the benefits of {@spell commune with nature}.

classFeature.name: Primal Resilience
entries: The mystical power in your blood increases your resiliency in times of need. From 10th level, if you fail a saving throw, you can expend an Adrenaline Surge to add the Constitution modifier of your normal form (a minimum of +1) to your roll, possibly turning a failure into a success.

classFeature.name: Primeval Form
entries: Starting at 11th level, when you Shift into a Bloodline Shape, you can infuse it with primeval power. So long as you remain in that Beast Shape, it gains these additional benefits:

items: The Beast takes on a primeval appearance, and grows by one size category, so long as there is room.
The Beast gains Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage from non-magical attacks.
The Beast has advantage on all ability checks and saving throws that use its Strength or Dexterity.

entries: You can transform into a Primeval Form three times. You regain one expended use when you finish a short rest, and all of your expended uses each time you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 12th level, you can increase one of your ability scores by 2, or two ability scores by 1. You cannot use this feature to increase any ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Bloodline Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Bloodline.

classFeature.name: Feral Senses
entries: You hunt as an apex predator, never losing track of your prey. Starting at 14th level, you can't have disadvantage on Natural Weapon attacks against targets within 30 feet of you.

classFeature.name: Mythic Forms
entries: At 15th level, you gain the ability to Shift into truly legendary Beasts of {@filter CR 5|bestiary|type=Beast|challenge rating=[&0;&5]|source=LLCompendiumOfBeasts}. However, you can only Shift into each Beast Shape you know of CR 5 and up, once per short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 16th level, you can increase one of your ability scores by 2, or two ability scores by 1. You cannot use this feature to increase any ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Primeval Resurgence
entries: In your blood flows a wellspring of primeval power. Starting at 17th level, you can gain the benefits of Primeval Form any time you use {@classFeature Wild Shape|Shifter|LLShifter|2|LLShifter} to Shift into a Bloodline Shape.

classFeature.name: Bloodline Feature
entries: You gain a feature from your Bloodline.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: At 19th level, you can increase one of your ability scores by 2, or two ability scores by 1. You cannot use this feature to increase any ability score above 20.

classFeature.name: Force of Nature
entries: Your primeval ferocity cannot be tamed. At 20th level, you can Shift into {@filter Beast Shapes with a CR of 5 or 6|bestiary|type=Beast|challenge rating=[&5;&6]|source=LLCompendiumOfBeasts} without limit.
You also regain one expended use of {@classFeature Adrenaline Surge|Shifter|LLShifter|6|LLShifter} at the start of each of your turns in combat.

subclassFeature.name: Avian Bloodline
entries: The great heights and winds call to you, as within your veins flows the primal power of great Beasts of the air. You have an affinity for birds of all kinds: from the smallest songbirds to the great predators of the sky, they are all your avian kin.

subclassFeature.name: Avian Shapes
entries: {@i 1st-level Avian Bloodline feature}

Your Bloodline has infused you with an instinctive knowledge of certain animals. All {@filter Beasts with the {@i Avian} creature subtype|bestiary|type=Beast|tag=Avian} are considered Bloodline Shapes for you.

subclassFeature.name: Avian Anatomy
entries: Your Bloodline infuses you with an avian grace, granting you certain Avian traits when you are not in Beast Shape:

entries.name: Featherweight Frame
entries: You can take the Dash action as a bonus action. Whenever you Dash, opportunity attacks made against you have disadvantage, and you have advantage on all Acrobatics checks for the rest of that turn.

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: Your light frame and feathers make you a difficult target. You gain Natural Armor with an Armor Class equal to 10 + your Constitution and Dexterity modifiers.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: Your fingers become pointed talons. They are Natural Weapons with the Light property that deal 1d6 Slashing damage on hit and use your Dexterity for their attack and damage rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Swift Wing
entries: {@i 7th-level Avian Bloodline feature}

The mystical power within your blood allows you to take to the skies even when you do not have wings. Whenever you take the Dash action in a form that does not have a flying speed, you gain a temporary flying speed equal to your walking speed until the end of your turn.
Also, while you have a flying speed, you have advantage on Dexterity saving throws.

subclassFeature.name: Driving Squall
entries: {@i 13th-level Avian Bloodline feature}

Your bond with the Beasts of the skies grants you control over the winds. While in a Bloodline Shape, you can use your bonus action to buffet enemies with wind. Choose either an adjacent 20-foot cone, a 30-foot by 5-foot line, or a 10-foot radius around you.
Creatures in that area must make a Strength saving throw or be thrown 20 feet away from you in a straight line. The distance is halved for each size a creature is larger than you.

subclassFeature.name: Master of the Winds
entries: {@i 18th-level Avian Bloodline feature}

You are a master of the that which blow across every world. As a bonus action, you can use Buffeting Winds to create the effect of the {@spell whirlwind (alternate)|llspell} spell in a space adjacent to you.

When you do, this effect uses your Shifter save DC: it does not require {@status concentration}, and you are immune to its effects. As a bonus action, you can use Buffeting Winds to move the {@spell whirlwind (alternate)|llspell} up to 30 feet so long as you are adjacent to it.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can conjure another {@spell whirlwind (alternate)|llspell}.

subclassFeature.name: Brute Bloodline
entries: In your blood flows the power of the great beasts of the wilds, infusing you with primal resilience. Some are gifted power by guardian Beasts, while others are granted primal abilities to serve as protectors of the smallest creatures. No matter their origin, Brutes are all as stalwart as the greatest behemoths.

subclassFeature.name: Brute Shapes
entries: {@i 1st-level Brute Bloodline feature}

Your Bloodline has infused you with an instinctive knowledge of certain animals. All {@filter Beasts with the {@i Brute} creature subtype|bestiary|type=Beast|tag=Brute} are considered Bloodline Shapes for you.

subclassFeature.name: Brutish Biology
entries: The power in your blood has made you hardy, granting you certain Brute traits when you are not in Beast Shape:

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: You grow woolly hair or thickened skin. You have a Natural Armor with an Armor Class that is equal to 10 + your Constitution and Strength modifiers.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: Your skull thickens, or you grow horns; Natural Weapons that deal 1d10 Bludgeoning damage on hit.

entries.name: Primal Fortitude
entries: Whenever you make a Strength check or saving throw, you can add your normal form's Constitution modifier (minimum of +1) to the result of your roll.

entries.name: Stout Frame
entries: Your hit point maximum increases by 1, and increases by 1 again each time you gain another Shifter level.

subclassFeature.name: Resilient Hide
entries: {@i 7th-level Brute Bloodline feature}

Your brutish Beasts are infused with mystical fortitude. Any time you start your turn in a Bloodline Shape, you can choose to gain temporary hit points equal to the Beast's Constitution modifier (minimum of 1).
Moreover, any time you have temporary hit points, you gain Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

subclassFeature.name: Stampede
entries: {@i 13th-level Brute Bloodline feature}
The primal power in your blood drives you to fits of anger. As an action, you can move up to your speed in a line, forcing all creatures you pass through to make a Strength saving throw.

On a failure, they take Bludgeoning damage based on your size, as shown below, and if the target is smaller than you, it falls Prone. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much Bludgeoning damage and do not fall Prone.

subclassFeature.name: Great Behemoth
entries: {@i 18th-level Brute Bloodline feature}

On rare occasions, you can manifest the full primal power of your Bloodline, transforming into a Great Behemoth. When you use {@classFeature Primeval Form|Shifter|LLShifter|11|LLShifter}, you can choose for the Beast Shape to become a Great Behemoth, gaining additional benefits:

items: It becomes Huge in size if it was not already.
Creatures smaller than Huge have disadvantage on their Strength saving throw to resist your {@subclassFeature Stampede|Shifter|LLShifter|Brute|LLShifter|13|LLShifter} feature.
Its Natural Weapon attacks deal a bonus {@damage 1d12} damage.
It is resistant to all damage except force and psychic.

entries: Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use your Great Behemoth form again.

subclassFeature.name: Carnivore Bloodline
entries: In your veins flows the blood of a primeval hunter. Often the descendants of powerful lycanthropes or shapeshifter Druids, these bestial warriors have an instinctive desire for blood and combat. Known for taking on the shapes of savage, predatory Beasts, Carnivore Shifters make terrifying foes in battle.

subclassFeature.name: Carnivore Shapes
entries: {@i 1st-level Carnivore Bloodline feature}

Your Bloodline has infused you with an instinctive knowledge of certain Beasts. {@filter Beasts with the {@i Carnivore} creature subtype|bestiary|type=Beast|tag=Carnivore} are considered Bloodline Shapes for you.

subclassFeature.name: Carnivorous Physique
entries: The primal drive to hunt alters your physiology, granting you certain Carnivore traits when you are not in Beast Shape:

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: Your body is hardened to resist your prey, giving you a Natural Armor with an Armor Class of 10 + your Strength and Constitution modifiers.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: You grow sharp claws or deadly fangs. They are Natural Weapons with both the Light and Finesse properties that deal 1d6 Slashing damage on hit.

entries.name: Primal Sense
entries: When you make an ability check based on your sense of smell, you treat a d20 roll of 7 or lower as an 8.

subclassFeature.name: Prey Drive
entries: Ancient instincts in your blood drive you to hunt. When you Shift into a Bloodline Shape, you can mark a Hostile creature you can see as your Prey. You can also spend an action while in a Bloodline Shape to analyze signs of a creature's passing (such as tracks, etc.) to mark it as your Prey.
It remains your Prey for 1 hour, until it is slain, you mark another creature as your Prey, or you leave that Beast Shape. For the duration, you gain certain benefits and drawbacks:

items: Your Natural Weapon attacks against your Prey score a critical hit on a d20 roll of 19 or 20, but you have disadvantage on attacks against all other targets.
When you make an ability check to track your Prey that uses your sense of smell, you have advantage on your roll.
Your walking speed increases by 10 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Bloodlust
entries: {@i 7th-level Carnivore Bloodline feature}

You unleash your ferocity when you taste blood. When you hit your Prey with a Natural Weapon attack, you can expend one Adrenaline Surge to enhance your Prey Drive. While it lasts, you have advantage on Natural Weapon attacks against your Prey, and you cannot be Grappled, Paralyzed, or Restrained.

subclassFeature.name: Apex Predator
entries: {@i 13th-level Carnivore Bloodline feature}

You are driven to pursue your foes until the hunt is finished. You have advantage on any saving throw your Prey forces you to make, and your Natural Weapon attacks against your Prey now score a critical hit on a d20 roll of 18-20.
At 18th level, this critical hit range increases to 17-20.

subclassFeature.name: Primeval Frenzy
entries: {@i 18th-level Carnivore Bloodline feature}

Ancient predatory instinct drives you to fight past the brink of death. If you are reduced to 0 hit points and Prey Drive is active, you don't fall Unconscious and can gain temporary hit points. Until you regain hit points, you continue to make death saving throws, dying if you fail three.

subclassFeature.name: Reptilian Bloodline
entries: Your blood carries the memories of ancient scales and claws. Whether you are the victim of a sinister curse or the chosen scion of a reptilian cult, your bloodline now bears the cold-blooded power of reptilians.

subclassFeature.name: Reptilian Shapes
entries: {@i 1st-level Reptilian Bloodline feature}

Your Bloodline has infused you with an instinctive knowledge of certain animals. {@filter Beasts with the {@i Reptilian} creature subtype|bestiary|type=Beast|tag=Reptilian} are considered Bloodline Shapes for you.

subclassFeature.name: Reptilian Physiology
entries: The power of your Bloodline grants you certain Reptilian traits when you are not in Beast Shape:

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: You are covered in a thin sheen of scales; Natural Armor granting you an Armor Class of 10 + your Dexterity and Constitution modifiers.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: Your hands grow elongated claws. They are Natural Weapons with the Light and Finesse properties that deal 1d6 slashing damage on hit.

entries.name: Sticky Grip
entries: You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and you can climb up difficult and sheer surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.

subclassFeature.name: Serpentine Stalker
entries: {@i 1st-level Reptilian Bloodline feature}

You move with the fluidity and silence of a reptilian predator. You gain proficiency in Stealth. If you are already proficient, whenever you make a Stealth check, you treat a d20 roll of 7 or lower as an 8.
In addition, when you are in a Bloodline Shape, you can take the Disengage action as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Adaptive Camouflage
entries: {@i 7th-level Reptilian Bloodline feature}

Your reptilian skin can adapt to blend into any environment. As an action, you can expend one use of {@classFeature Adrenaline Surge|Shifter|LLShifter|6|LLShifter} to turn {@condition invisible} for 10 minutes, as if by the {@spell invisibility} spell.
Unlike the spell, this ability does not require {@status concentration}.

subclassFeature.name: Coiled Strike
entries: You strike at your foes with deadly primeval speed. When you are in a Bloodline Shape, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have advantage on opportunity attacks.
After a creature you can sense attacks you, you can use your reaction to make an opportunity attack against it.
If you have already used your reaction for a round, you can expend an Adrenaline Surge to take another reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Crushing Might
entries: {@i 13th-level Reptilian Bloodline feature}

You crush your prey with the primeval force. When a creature starts its turn Grappled or Restrained by you, it must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it takes Bludgeoning damage equal to your level and has disadvantage on all ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws it makes on that turn.
If you are in a Bloodline Shape, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on this Strength saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Primeval Predator
entries: {@i 18th-level Reptilian Bloodline feature}
You master the predatory traits evolved by reptiles over the ages. You gain a special Blindsight that allows you to detect heat emitted by anything within 120 feet of you.
Finally, you gain a second reaction that you can use each round. However, you can only use one reaction per trigger.

subclassFeature.name: Vermin Bloodline
entries: Always present, but rarely seen, Vermin crawl, climb, and dig through every environment. You are bound by blood to these smallest of critters, able to take their shape and channel their humble power to overwhelm even the mightiest foes.

subclassFeature.name: Vermin Shapes
entries: {@i 1st-level Vermin Bloodline feature}

Your Bloodline infuses you with an instinctive knowledge of certain animals. {@filter Beasts with the {@i Vermin} creature subtype|bestiary|type=Beast|tag=Vermin} are considered Bloodline Shapes for you.

subclassFeature.name: Verminous Anatomy
entries: The power of your Bloodline grants you certain Vermin traits when you are not in Beast Shape:

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You gain Darkvision out to a 60-foot radius. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 60 feet.

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: Your skin is covered in thin, wiry fur, that gives you a Natural Armor with an Armor Class equal to 10 + your Constitution and Dexterity modifiers.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: Your teeth transform into thick, gnarled fangs. They are Natural Weapons with the Finesse and Light properties that deal 1d6 Piercing damage on hit.

entries.name: Scamper
entries: When you Shift into a Bloodline Shape, you also gain the benefits of either the Disengage or the Hide action.

subclassFeature.name: Plague Bearer
entries: {@i 1st-level Vermin Bloodline feature}
Your bloodline carries ancient plague and disease. When you hit a creature with a Natural Weapon attack, it cannot regain hit points until the beginning of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Swarm Shape
entries: Your mystical bond with the smallest of vermin allows you to join their swarms. You can learn Beast Shapes and Shift into Swarms so long as they are Vermin Swarms. You also learn a single Vermin Swarm Beast Shape of your choice. When you Shift into a Vermin Swarm, you use the following rules:

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to twice your Shifter level + your Constitution modifier. If these temporary hit points are reduced to 0, you instantly revert back to your normal form, then take any remaining damage.
When you use the Swarm's Flurry attack, you use these temporary hit points to determine the damage dealt by the Flurry attack, in place of your current hit points.
The Swarm's Flurry attack counts as a Natural Weapon attack for the purposes of your Shifter features and your Plague Bearer feature.

entries: You can Shift into a Swarm of Vermin once in between each short or long rest at no cost. However, after that, you must expend an Adrenaline Surge to Shift into a Swarm of Vermin again.

subclassFeature.name: Unseen Infiltrators
entries: {@i 13th-level Vermin Bloodline feature}

When you use Wild Awareness, you gain the knowledge and understanding of all Vermin within a 3-mile radius. This can be used in settlements, caves, and wilds. It reveals pathways, hiding spots, locations of dangerous creatures, and traps.

subclassFeature.name: Overwhelming Horde
entries: {@i 18th-level Vermin Bloodline feature}
You can overwhelm foes with a great multitude of Vermin. As an action, one creature you can see within 30 feet is swarmed by a Vermin horde, and must make a Dexterity saving throw.

On a failed save, it is covered by Vermin, and until it takes damage equal to twice your level, its speed is halved, and it has disadvantage on attack rolls, ability checks, and Dexterity saving throws. On a success, it is still covered, and must only take damage equal to your level for this effect to end.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can all upon the Overwhelming Horde again.

subclassFeature.name: Insect Bloodline
entries: Your blood is bound to all insects, arachnids, and arthropods, creatures which creep, crawl, and sting. Strangest of Shifters, you use your insectoid power to entrap and incapacitate foes.

subclassFeature.name: Insect Shapes
entries: {@i 1st-level Insect Bloodline feature}

Your Bloodline infuses you with an instinctive knowledge of certain animals. {@filter Beasts with the {@i Insect} creature subtype|bestiary|type=Beast|tag=Insect} are considered Bloodline Shapes for you.

subclassFeature.name: Insectoid Evolution
entries: Your Bloodline infuses you with insectoid power, granting you certain Insect traits when you are not in Beast Shape:

entries.name: Arachnoid Grip
entries: You gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed, and you can climb difficult and sheer surfaces, including upside down, without making an ability check.

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: Your flesh is covered in a hard, insectoid carapace, granting you a Natural Armor with an Armor Class equal to 10 + your Constitution and Dexterity modifiers.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: Your hands grow wicked stingers. They are Natural Weapons with the Finesse property that deal 1d8 Piercing damage on hit.

subclassFeature.name: Stinging Strike
entries: {@i 1st-level Insect Bloodline feature}

The power in your blood causes your body to produce potent toxins. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a Natural Weapon attack, you can force it to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes 1d8 bonus Poison damage and is Poisoned until the start of your next turn.
The damage of Stinging Strike increases by 1d8 when you reach 7th level (2d8), 13th level (3d8), and 18th level (4d8).

subclassFeature.name: Corrosive Stings
entries: Your toxins are infused with primal power. When one of your Bloodline or Beast Shape features deals Poison damage, you can choose for it to deal Acid damage instead.

subclassFeature.name: Swarm Shape
entries: Your mystical bond with the smallest of insects allows you to join their swarms. You can learn Beast Shapes and Shift into Swarms so long as they are Insect Swarms. When you Shift into an Insect Swarm, you use the following rules:

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to twice your Shifter level + your Constitution modifier. If these temporary hit points are reduced to 0, you instantly revert back to your normal form, then take any remaining damage.
When you use the Swarm's Flurry attack, you use these temporary hit points to determine the damage dealt by that Flurry attack, in place of your current hit points.

entries: You can Shift into an Insect Swarm once in between each short or long rest at no cost, after which, you must expend an Adrenaline Surge to Shift into an Insect Swarm again.

subclassFeature.name: Iridescent Escape
entries: {@i 13th-level Insect Bloodline feature}

You can channel insectoid power to escape danger. When you expend an Adrenaline Surge, you can move up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space without provoking opportunity attacks. As part of this reaction, you can Shift into a Bloodline Shape.

subclassFeature.name: Deadly Neurotoxin
entries: {@i 18th-level Insect Bloodline feature}

Your primeval toxins afflict body and mind. When you force a creature to make a saving throw to resist being Poisoned, you can change it to an Intelligence saving throw, and if the target fails by 5 or more, it is Paralyzed instead of Poisoned.

subclassFeature.name: Aquatic Bloodline
entries: You have formed a mystical bond with all Beasts of the water. From the smallest tide pool to the crushing deeps of the sea, you are familiar with its multitude of life. Drawing upon their power, you have become a savage warrior of the waves.

subclassFeature.name: Aquatic Shapes
entries: {@i 1st-level Aquatic Bloodline feature}
Your Bloodline infuses you with an instinctive knowledge of certain animals. All {@filter Beasts with the {@i Aquatic} creature subtype|bestiary|type=Beast|tag=Aquatic} are Bloodline Shapes for you.

subclassFeature.name: Aquatic Adaptation
entries: The power of your Bloodline alters physiology, granting you certain Aquatic traits when you are not in Beast Shape:

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You gain Darkvision out to a 60-foot radius. If you are underwater, the range of your Darkvision is doubled.

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: Your flesh is covered in a hard, Aquaticoid carapace, granting you a Natural Armor with an Armor Class equal to 10 + your Constitution and Dexterity modifiers.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: Your hands grow wicked stingers. They are Natural Weapons with the Finesse property that deal 1d8 Piercing damage on hit.

entries.name: Oceanic Resilience
entries: You gain Resistance to Cold damage and Immunity to the negative effects of deep water.

entries.name: Piscine Physiology
entries: You can breathe both air and water, and gain a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.

subclassFeature.name: Crushing Force
entries: You wield the overwhelming pressure of the depths. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a Natural Weapon attack, you can force it to make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it is knocked back 10 feet in a line, or its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of its next turn. Targets larger than you have advantage on this saving throw.
The potency of both effects increases as you gain levels in this class: at 13th level (20 feet) and 18th level (30 feet).

subclassFeature.name: Mystical Waters
entries: {@i 13th-level Aquatic Bloodline feature}

When you Shift into a Bloodline Shape, you can expend an Adrenaline Surge to infuse it with mystical power. For the duration, it has a flying speed equal to its swimming speed, and it treats the air as water for the purposes of its Traits.

subclassFeature.name: Terror of the Deep
entries: {@i 18th-level Aquatic Bloodline feature}

You are an inevitable force of the depths. While you are in a Bloodline Shape underwater, the area within 60 feet of you is difficult terrain for creatures of your choice. When you use Mystical Waters, this radius is halved to 30 feet.
Finally, if a creature fails its saving throw against Crushing Force while underwater, you can forgo its normal effects to instead cause the creature to suffer 1 level of Exhaustion.
